seekers.name: Jane Smith
seekers.headline: Senior Application Security Engineer
seekers.skills: Application Security, OWASP Top 10, Penetration Testing, Python, JavaScript, React, Node.js, AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, SAST/DAST, Threat Modeling, Security Code Review
seekers.about:
## About Me

I'm a passionate Application Security Engineer with 8 years of experience securing web applications and cloud infrastructure. I specialize in integrating security into the SDLC, conducting security assessments, and building security automation tools.

I'm deeply involved in the OWASP community and have contributed to several projects including OWASP ZAP and the OWASP Top 10. I believe in making security accessible and practical for development teams.

When I'm not breaking things (professionally), I enjoy contributing to open source, writing technical blog posts, and speaking at security conferences.

## Work Experience

**Senior Application Security Engineer** at **TechCorp Inc** (Jan 2022 - Present)
- Lead security assessments for 20+ microservices and web applications
- Implemented automated security testing in CI/CD pipeline, reducing vulnerabilities by 65%
- Designed and delivered security training program for 100+ developers
- Built internal security tools using Python and Go for vulnerability management
- Conducted threat modeling sessions for new product features

**Application Security Engineer** at **SecureStart** (Mar 2019 - Dec 2021)
- Performed security code reviews and penetration testing for client applications
- Developed SAST/DAST integration for multiple CI/CD platforms
- Created security documentation and secure coding guidelines
- Mentored junior security engineers and interns

**Security Analyst** at **CyberDefense LLC** (Jun 2016 - Feb 2019)
- Conducted vulnerability assessments and security audits
- Responded to security incidents and performed forensic analysis
- Implemented security monitoring and alerting systems
- Collaborated with development teams on security remediation

## Projects & Portfolio

- **SecureCI** - Open source security scanning orchestrator for CI/CD pipelines [GitHub](https://github.com/janesmith/secureci) | [Docs](https://secureci.dev)
- **OWASP ZAP** - Core contributor, added 15+ scanning rules and improved API security testing capabilities
- **VulnTracker** - Vulnerability management dashboard built with React and Node.js [Demo](https://vulntracker-demo.com)
- **Security Blog** - Technical blog with 50+ articles on application security [Blog](https://janesmith.dev/blog)

## Certifications & Education

- OSCP (Offensive Security Certified Professional) - 2023
- CISSP (Certified Information Systems Security Professional) - 2021
- CEH (Certified Ethical Hacker) - 2019
- AWS Certified Security - Specialty - 2022
- M.S. Cybersecurity, Stanford University - 2018
- B.S. Computer Science, MIT - 2016

## Work Samples

- Blog: "Securing React Applications: A Comprehensive Guide" - https://janesmith.dev/blog/securing-react-apps
- Talk: "Building Security into DevOps" at DEF CON 30 - https://youtube.com/watch?v=example
- Research: "Novel Techniques for Detecting XSS in Modern SPAs" - https://arxiv.org/example
- Podcast: "The Security Engineer's Mindset" on Security Weekly - https://securityweekly.com/jane-smith
- Workshop: "Hands-on Web Application Security" at OWASP Global AppSec - https://github.com/janesmith/appsec-workshop

## What I'm Looking For

I'm seeking a Senior or Lead Application Security Engineer role at a company that values security as a core principle, not an afterthought. Ideal opportunities would involve:

- Building and leading security programs from the ground up
- Working with modern cloud-native architectures (Kubernetes, serverless, etc.)
- Collaborating with engineering teams to build security into the development process
- Contributing to open source security tools and projects
- Mentoring and growing a security team

I'm particularly interested in companies in the fintech, healthcare, or developer tools space. Remote work is preferred, but I'm open to hybrid arrangements for the right opportunity.

Salary expectations: $180k - $220k depending on location and benefits.

seekers.name: Jonathan Mmadu
seekers.headline: Software Developer
seekers.skills: TypeScript, JavaScript (ES6+), React, Next.js, Vue.js, Tailwind CSS, SCSS, Node.js (Express), Redux, HTMX, Monorepos (Turborepo), Git/GitHub, Figma MCP workflows
seekers.about:
## About Me

I am a Software Developer driven by the challenge of building functional, high-impact applications. With over three years of experience evolving from frontend foundations to full-stack logic, I've found my rhythm in crafting software that truly works for people. Whether I’m structuring a monorepo for a complex project or contributing to open-source initiatives, my goal is always the same: clean, predictable code that makes life easier for both the user and my fellow developers.

Beyond the terminal, I am an avid reader who finds just as much satisfaction in a complex mystery novel or an adventure tale as I do in solving a difficult bug. I treat every tricky technical challenge like a case that needs solving, and I don't stop until the logic is airtight. I’m a clear, collaborative communicator who believes that the best code is built with empathy and a curious mind.

## Work Experience

**Fullstack Developer** at **Hawkish Group** Feb. 2025 - Feb. 2026)
- Architected and maintained scalable web applications, bridging responsive frontend interfaces with robust backend logic.
- Optimized system performance by refactoring API data fetching and state management, ensuring high reliability and secure data handling complex operational tasks.

**Frontend Developer** at **Dosh Services** (Jan. 2024 - Dec. 2024)
- Engineered dynamic web applications by integrating React and Next.js interfaces with backend REST APIs for seamless, real-time data flow.
- Refactored legacy codebases using Tailwind CSS and SCSS, significantly optimizing web application performance and browser loading speeds.
-  Collaborated with designers to translate high-fidelity Figma prototypes into pixel-perfect, responsive components.

**Open Source Contributor** at **OWASP Foundation** (2026 - Present)
- Assisted with UI refinements, course filtering logic, and minor fixes during the BLT University platform's architectural transition.
- Overhauled the mobile navigation drawer and improved UI responsiveness for the BLT Jobs platform.
- Drafted technical proposals and implementation plans for Google Summer of Code (GSoC) 2026.

**Frontend Intern** at **HNG9 Internship** (Oct. 2022 - Dec. 2022)
- Collaborated in an agile, high-pressure environment with over 50 cross-functional team members (DevOps, Backend, Product).
- Developed the interface for an AI-adjacent audio analysis tool, allowing users to transcribe and analyze call recordings.

## Projects & Portfolio

- **OWASP BLT Ecosystem** - Open Source Contribution - Focused on mobile architecture, UI accessibility, and course filtering logic for BLT Jobs and BLT University.
- **BizTalk** - A centralized media platform built with a Next.js monorepo architecture to handle diverse data streams like live TV and radio.
- **Medinova** - A medical application backend built with Express.js and MongoDB with a focus on robust authentication.

## Certifications & Education

-  HNG9 Internship (HNG9 FINALIST CERTIFICATE) - 2023
- DevFest Lagos (Certificate of Contribution) - 2024

## Work Samples

<!-- Share links to your work samples, blog posts, talks, or publications

Example:
- Blog: "Securing Node.js Applications" - https://yourblog.com/post
- Talk: "OWASP Top 10 Deep Dive" at BSides 2023 - https://youtube.com/watch?v=...
- Research: "Novel XSS Detection Techniques" - https://paper-link.com
-->

## What I'm Looking For

I am actively seeking a remote Software Engineering or Frontend Internship where I can contribute to building scalable web applications and distributed systems. I thrive in diverse, collaborative environments where I can leverage my analytical problem-solving skills to engineer high-quality, user-centric software. I am particularly interested in teams that value open-source contributions, modern architectures (like monorepos and edge computing), and an empathetic approach to product development.

---

seekers.name: Mohamed Aziz Rebhi
seekers.headline: Software Engineering Student (AI & Backend Focus)
seekers.skills: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, SQL, FastAPI, REST APIs, JSON-RPC, Docker, Docker Compose, Redis, Celery, GitHub Actions, PostgreSQL, pgvector, MySQL, SQLite, LangGraph, LangChain, RAG Pipelines, HuggingFace, Ollama, MCP Protocol, Async Programming, System Design Basics, CI/CD, Local LLM Deployment, Model Benchmarking
seekers.about:
## About Me

Computer Science Engineering student at ENSI specializing in backend systems and AI infrastructure, with a strong focus on LLM deployment and distributed AI systems. I have built production-grade RAG pipelines, MCP servers, and agentic AI systems, combining backend engineering with modern AI workflows.

During my internships, I worked on real-world AI infrastructure problems including retrieval systems, evaluation pipelines, and local model inference. I enjoy building systems that integrate APIs, background processing, and intelligent data pipelines, with an emphasis on reliability and scalability.

I am currently focused on strengthening my system design and problem-solving skills while preparing for backend engineering roles. My goal is to become a strong engineer capable of designing and operating real-world AI-powered systems.

## Work Experience

**Software Developer Intern — AI Infrastructure** at **Sofrecom Tunisie** (Feb 2026 - Apr 2026)
- Built a production RAG system using LangGraph, Celery, PostgreSQL/pgvector, and bi-encoder reranking  
- Implemented RAGAS evaluation pipeline for measuring retrieval and generation quality  
- Deployed full stack using Docker Compose with asynchronous Celery task queues  

**Software Developer Intern — MCP & LLM Integration** at **Sofrecom Tunisie** (Jul 2025 - Aug 2025)
- Developed a custom MCP server for PostgreSQL enabling natural language to SQL query generation  
- Benchmarked local LLM inference using Ollama (DeepSeek models)  
- Integrated MCP workflows with GitHub Copilot for developer-facing AI tooling  

**Software Developer Intern** at **Actia Engineering Services** (Jul 2024 - Sep 2024)
- Enhanced a survey web application using Angular and Spring Boot  
- Optimized backend CRUD operations and improved application performance

## Projects & Portfolio

- **ProofOfWork** - Developer productivity tracking platform using FastAPI, PostgreSQL, pgvector, Celery, Redis, and Docker  
- Integrated GitHub Webhooks to capture developer activity and sessions  
- Implemented JWT authentication and semantic search  
- Reduced latency by 90% using Redis caching  

- **LangGraph Essay Writer** - Agentic AI system for multi-step reasoning workflows  
- Built using LangGraph, Tavily, Supabase, and Streamlit  

- **Open Source Contribution (OWASP BLT)**  
- 9 merged PRs across multiple repositories including MCP handler refactor, API improvements, and authentication fixes

## Certifications & Education

- Engineering Degree in Computer Science, ENSI (National School of Computer Science), Tunisia - Expected October 2026  
- Preparatory Cycle in Mathematics and Physics, Monastir - 2021–2023  
- Agentic AI Course  
- AI Agents in LangGraph

## Work Samples

- GitHub: https://github.com/azizrebhi  
- LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/in/aziz-rebhi

## What I'm Looking For

I am currently looking for internship opportunities in backend engineering or software engineering, especially roles involving APIs, distributed systems, and applied AI.

I am particularly interested in environments where I can work on real production systems, contribute to meaningful engineering challenges, and continue growing in backend and infrastructure development. I am open to startups, mid-size companies, and large tech organizations.

---

I'm actively involved in the open-source community, where I enjoy building optimized, high-impact tools that bridge the gap between complex architecture and practical execution.

Actively contributing to real-world, open-source codebases is a powerful catalyst for sustained technical growth. This dual-impact approach ensures that while you are continuously expanding your own engineering capabilities, you are simultaneously delivering tangible optimizations that improve the functionality and reliability of existing software.
